Technology.thesis: The AI infrastructure buildout continues to be the dominant driver, with strong demand for chips, data centers, and related services. Software companies are also pivoting towards AI integration, though some face workforce displacement concerns.
watchFor: Mag 7 hyperscaler capex guide DOWN at next earnings — would crack the AI demand thesis
Hot core CPI print > 0.4% MoM — forces a hawkish Fed repricing, killing duration bid for software
China export controls expand to HBM or advanced packaging — impacts global chip supply chain

Consumer Cyclical.thesis: E-commerce continues to show resilience and growth, but broader retail faces headwinds from rising fuel prices and cautious consumer sentiment. Residential construction remains under pressure.
watchFor: Hot core CPI print > 0.4% MoM — further dampens consumer spending
Significant drop in gasoline prices — eases pressure on retailers and consumers
Fed signals rate cuts — could boost housing market activity

Communication Services.thesis: The sector is seeing a shift towards new content strategies and monetization, particularly in streaming and digital advertising, but some areas face regulatory scrutiny or competitive pressures.
watchFor: Major streaming service reports subscriber decline
New regulatory action against large tech platforms (e.g., antitrust)
Significant downturn in global advertising spend

Industrials.thesis: Defense spending remains robust due to geopolitical tensions, while the commercial space economy is gaining traction. However, some industrial sub-sectors face mixed demand signals.
watchFor: De-escalation of major geopolitical conflicts — reduces defense spending
Significant cuts to government space budgets
Global economic slowdown impacting industrial machinery demand

Healthcare.thesis: The GLP-1 obesity drug wave continues to drive significant interest and growth, while healthcare plans remain strong. Medical device innovation also contributes positively.
watchFor: New, unexpected side effects emerge from GLP-1 drugs
Increased regulatory scrutiny on drug pricing or approval processes
Major shifts in healthcare policy impacting insurance or device reimbursement

Financials.thesis: Major banks have cleared regulatory hurdles, and credit services continue to show strength. Asset management firms are also seeing momentum, though some areas like capital markets have mixed signals.
watchFor: Unexpected Fed hawkish pivot (e.g., rate hikes resume) — hurts duration and credit
Significant increase in loan defaults or credit card delinquencies
New, burdensome financial regulations from SEC or Fed

Consumer Defensive.thesis: The sector is navigating a challenging consumer environment, with some companies showing resilience while others face headwinds from inflation and changing consumer preferences.
watchFor: Hot core CPI print > 0.4% MoM — further erodes consumer purchasing power
Significant drop in inflation — could boost discretionary spending within defensive categories
Major shifts in consumer brand loyalty or private label adoption

Utilities.thesis: The utilities sector, particularly independent power producers, is benefiting from the surge in AI data center demand for reliable and increasing power supply.
watchFor: Slowing AI data center buildouts or efficiency gains reducing power demand
New regulations impacting power generation or transmission costs
Significant shifts in energy prices (e.g., natural gas)

Basic Materials.thesis: The industrial metals and mining sector is heavily influenced by geopolitical dynamics and supply chain considerations, particularly for critical materials.
watchFor: De-escalation of geopolitical tensions impacting rare earth supply
New discoveries of critical mineral deposits outside of current dominant producers
Significant downturn in global industrial production

Precious Metals.thesis: Gold is benefiting from geopolitical uncertainty and ceasefire hopes, while silver faces headwinds from a significant price decline. The broader inflation narrative from recent CPI/PPI prints could also be a factor.
watchFor: De-escalation of geopolitical conflicts — reduces safe-haven demand for gold
Stronger-than-expected economic data (e.g., jobs, GDP) — reduces demand for safe havens
Significant strengthening of the US Dollar — weighs on precious metals
